chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The excess in the expenditure for the Hajj

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/170)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/170)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ ( صلوات الله عليه ) لَيَنْقَطِعُ رِكَابُهُ فِي طَرِيقِ مَكَّةَ فَيَشُدُّهُ بِخُوصَةٍ لِيُهَوِّنَ الْحَجَّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Rabie Bin Abdullah who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> was, if his<sup>asws</sup> ride (stirrup) was cut off in the road to Makkah, he<sup>asws</sup> would tighten it especially to ease the Hajj upon himself<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Ali from Rib’iy ibn ‘Abd Allah who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘On his way to al-Hajj, ’Amir al-Mu 5 minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would mend a damaged stirrup of his transportation animal with palm tree fibers to make al-Hajj easier for him. ‘“
